name: Tokyo
localTips: Get a Suica or Pasmo card (or add one to your phone wallet) — it works on every train, bus, vending machine and konbini.
Eating while walking is frowned upon — stand to the side or find a bench. Konbini have eat-in corners.
Trains stop around midnight and taxis are pricey — plan your last train or commit to staying out until the 5am first train.
Cash still matters at small restaurants and shrines — 7-Eleven ATMs reliably accept foreign cards.
Queueing is the national sport: a line outside a tiny ramen shop is the best restaurant review you'll ever get.

activities.title: Shibuya Crossing & Hachikō
activities.description: Cross the world's busiest intersection, then watch the choreography from above — the Shibuya Sky corner or the free Mag's Park rooftop.

activities.title: Sensō-ji temple & Nakamise-dōri
activities.description: Tokyo's oldest temple behind the great red lantern of Kaminarimon — browse the snack stalls of Nakamise on the way in, then find the quiet garden to the west.

activities.title: Meiji Shrine forest
activities.description: A 100,000-tree forest swallows the city sounds on the walk to the shrine — watch for wedding processions and write a wish on an ema plaque.

activities.title: Tokyo Metropolitan Government free observatory
activities.description: A free 202-metre observation deck in Shinjuku — on clear winter days Mount Fuji floats on the horizon at sunset.

activities.title: Yanaka old-town streets
activities.description: The Tokyo that survived — temple lanes, cats, craft shops and the retro Yanaka Ginza shopping street with its famous sunset steps.

activities.title: Shinjuku Gyoen garden
activities.description: Three gardens in one — Japanese, French and English lawns — minutes from the world's busiest station. ¥500 buys half a day of calm.

activities.title: Akihabara electric town & arcades
activities.description: Multi-storey arcades, retro game shops and anime towers — browse Super Potato's floors of vintage consoles even if you buy nothing.

activities.title: Tsukiji Outer Market breakfast
activities.description: The famous inner auction moved to Toyosu, but the outer market still grills scallops, rolls tamagoyaki and pours morning sushi like nowhere else.

activities.title: Harajuku: Takeshita Street to Omotesandō
activities.description: Rainbow crepes and teen fashion chaos, then the plane-tree avenue of flagship architecture — Tokyo's two fashion universes in one walk.

activities.title: Imperial Palace East Gardens
activities.description: The shogun's castle foundations, samurai guardhouses and immaculate seasonal plantings — free, central and strangely uncrowded.

activities.title: Shimokitazawa vintage crawl
activities.description: Tokyo's thrift-store capital — a tangle of car-free lanes stuffed with vintage shops, record stores and coffee counters.

activities.title: Depachika basement food halls
activities.description: Beneath Isetan and Mitsukoshi lie food halls of jewel-box wagashi, ¥10,000 melons and endless free samples — Japanese food culture in one basement.

activities.title: Zōjō-ji temple with Tokyo Tower backdrop
activities.description: The 17th-century temple gate frames Tokyo Tower's orange lattice — the city's best old-meets-new photograph, especially at dusk.

activities.title: Omoide Yokochō lantern alleys
activities.description: 'Memory Lane' — a smoky warren of six-seat yakitori counters under the Shinjuku tracks, unchanged since the 1940s.
activities.insiderNote: Look for shops with prices displayed; a seat charge (~¥300–400) is normal. Point at what your neighbor is eating and hold up fingers.

activities.title: Golden Gai bar hop
activities.description: Two hundred bars the size of closets in six alleys — each with its own theme, regulars and five stools. Pick a door with a sign you like.
activities.insiderNote: Bars with English signs and no cover charge welcome first-timers; 'members only' means exactly that. Start after 21:00.

activities.title: Shōwa-era kissaten coffee
activities.description: Time-capsule coffee houses like Chatei Hatou in Shibuya — velvet chairs, hand-dripped single origins and absolute quiet since the 1970s.
activities.insiderNote: Order the 'morning set' before 11:00: thick shokupan toast, boiled egg and coffee for the price of the coffee alone.

activities.title: Neighborhood sentō bathhouse
activities.description: Tokyo's public baths survive in every ward — mosaic Fuji murals, scalding tubs and post-bath coffee milk drunk hands-on-hips.
activities.insiderNote: Tattoos are usually fine at small sentō (unlike onsen resorts). Bring a small towel or rent one; wash seated before entering the tub.

activities.title: Nakameguro canal & coffee
activities.description: A tree-lined canal of low-key boutiques and the cathedral-like Starbucks Reserve Roastery — Tokyo's most relaxed afternoon neighborhood.
activities.insiderNote: During cherry blossom season the canal becomes a pink tunnel with evening lantern lighting — go on a weeknight to breathe.

activities.title: Standing soba at a tachigui counter
activities.description: Order from the ticket machine, slurp audibly, done in eight minutes — the salaryman lunch ritual at station stand-up soba counters.
activities.insiderNote: Kakiage (mixed tempura fritter) on kake soba is the classic order — the whole meal runs ¥400–600.

activities.title: Daikanyama T-Site book village
activities.description: Three linked pavilions of books, vinyl listening booths and a lounge bar among the trees — routinely called the world's most beautiful bookstore.
activities.insiderNote: The Anjin lounge upstairs serves drinks amid vintage magazine archives — a gorgeous rainy-evening hideout most tourists never find.

activities.title: teamLab Planets immersive museum
activities.description: Wade barefoot through knee-deep mirror water among floating orchids and infinite light — Tokyo's must-book digital art experience.

activities.title: Shibuya Sky observation deck
activities.description: An open-air rooftop 229 metres above the Crossing — lie back on the netted corner and watch the city grid pulse below. Book the sunset slot.
activities.bookingQuery: Shibuya Sky observation deck ticket Tokyo

activities.title: Sushi-making class with a chef
activities.description: Learn proper shari rice, knife angles and nigiri pressure from a working sushi chef — then eat your own omakase.
activities.bookingQuery: sushi making class with chef Tokyo

activities.title: Asakusa rickshaw tour
activities.description: Pulled through Asakusa's backstreets by athlete-guides who narrate, pose you for photos and know every hidden shrine — pure fun.

activities.title: Mount Fuji & Hakone day trip
activities.description: Lake Kawaguchi viewpoints, the Hakone ropeway over volcanic springs and — weather willing — the perfect cone itself.
activities.bookingQuery: Mount Fuji Hakone day trip from Tokyo

activities.title: Nighttime izakaya & ramen tour
activities.description: A local guide steers you through Shinjuku's izakaya maze — yakitori, highballs and a final midnight ramen you'd never find alone.
activities.bookingQuery: izakaya food tour Shinjuku Tokyo night

activities.title: Sumo morning practice viewing
activities.description: Watch rikishi collide at a stable's morning keiko from arm's length — silent, sweaty and unforgettable. Strict etiquette, worth every rule.
activities.bookingQuery: sumo morning practice tour Ryogoku Tokyo

activities.title: Tokyo Skytree fast entry
activities.description: The 634-metre broadcast tower's twin decks turn the megacity into circuitry — on clear days the view runs 70 km to Fuji.
activities.bookingQuery: Tokyo Skytree admission ticket fast entry

activities.title: Kimono rental day in Asakusa
activities.description: Dressed by professionals (hair styling included), wander Sensō-ji and the old streets in full kimono — photos you'll frame.

activities.title: Cherry blossoms in Ueno Park
activities.description: A thousand somei-yoshino trees canopy the park's central path — join a hanami picnic under the petals like the whole city does.

activities.title: Sumida River fireworks festival
activities.description: Twenty thousand fireworks over the river in late July, watched in yukata with festival food — Japan's oldest hanabi tradition.
